intervention_inventory.summary: Champion proposed Split-K reasoning assuming BW-bound kernels benefit from more CTAs, but 40 CTAs already provides sufficient DRAM saturation

intervention_inventory.summary: Champion assumed 15pp production BW gap is proportional (additive to kernel improvement), but it is from absolute time in non-GEMM transitions. Corrected E2E from 10-12% to 4-8%.

citation.quoted_content: ISSUE FLAGGED (WARNING): Production BW impact model is flawed. The 15pp gap is from absolute time in non-GEMM transitions (1.97ms), not proportional. Warp specialization improves isolated BW but doesn't eliminate transition dead time.

citation.quoted_content: [WARNING SENT] Delegate prompt says 'F.linear is the production dispatch path' — but F.linear dispatches to cuBLAS in standalone scripts. The actual Triton skinny GEMM is torch.ops.vllm.triton_skinny_gemm.

intervention_inventory.summary: Section 3c baseline mismatch: down_proj cited as 538 GB/s (persistent bench) vs 686.4 GB/s (splitk bench) — 1.236x speedup claim likely artifact of slower baseline. Champion retracted claim.

intervention_inventory.summary: Cold-cache claim is cornerstone of proposal but unverified — only one BLOCK_K=128 experiment showing zero cold benefit, not a general characterization

citation.claim: Monitor flagged that cold-cache claim was unverified — champion then ran diagnostic experiment first
citation.quoted_content: ISSUE FLAGGED [WARNING]: Cold-cache claim is cornerstone of proposal but unverified. Champion cites BLOCK_K=128 experiment showing '1.40x warm-cache but zero cold-cache benefit' as evidence that Triton is warm-cache-optimized

intervention_inventory.summary: Test A warm-cache measures L2 bandwidth (1171-3605 GB/s >> 864 GB/s DRAM peak), not DRAM. Warm/cold ratio 3.81x is L2-vs-DRAM, scientifically meaningless for production gap analysis.

citation.claim: Monitor sent CRITICAL that Test A measures L2 bandwidth not DRAM, warm/cold ratio 3.81x is meaningless
citation.quoted_content: CRITICAL issue flagged: Test A (warm cache) measures L2 bandwidth (1171-3605 GB/s >> 864 GB/s DRAM peak), not warm-DRAM bandwidth. The tight CUDA graph loop on same weight tensor fits in L2 — never touches DRAM.

intervention_inventory.summary: CUDA C++ prototype timing showed 65498 GB/s (7580%) — impossible, likely CUDA graph measurement capturing only tiny copy. Champion excluded from proposal.

citation.claim: Monitor caught impossible CUDA C++ timing of 7580% BW utilization, likely CUDA graph measurement artifact
citation.quoted_content: CRITICAL ISSUE: CUDA C++ timing invalid again — 65,498 GB/s (7580%) impossible. Likely cause: `wrap_cuda` creates new tensor inside CUDA graph capture; measurement captures only the tiny copy not the DRAM kernel

intervention_inventory.summary: Champion planned to use full FP8 (torch._scaled_mm, both operands) as proxy for weight-only FP8. Addressed — impact immaterial at M=8.

citation.quoted_content: [WARNING sent] Champion planned to use full FP8 (torch._scaled_mm, both operands) as proxy for weight-only FP8. [ADDRESSED] Champion correctly assessed quantitative impact is immaterial at M=8 (<0.05% traffic difference)

intervention_inventory.summary: Monitor flagged confounded Tier 1 model using cross-shape data. Champion already noting model issues, correctly pivoting away from Split-K after 0.40-0.65x measured regressions.

intervention_inventory.summary: Champion incorrectly stated L40S supports FP8 tensor cores. L40S is SM89 (Ada), FP8 MMA was introduced in Hopper (SM90). Champion self-corrected to BF16 MMA with FP8-to-BF16 dequant.

citation.claim: Monitor sent CRITICAL that L40S SM89 has no FP8 tensor cores, champion corrected to BF16 MMA with dequant
citation.quoted_content: CRITICAL ISSUE FOUND: Champion incorrectly states 'L40S supports FP8 tensor cores' multiple times in thinking block. L40S is Ada Lovelace (SM89) — FP8 MMA was introduced in Hopper (SM90).

intervention_inventory.summary: FP8 E4M3 max relative error 3488x flagged as potential correctness bug. Later corrected — high relative error is metrics artifact from near-zero outputs, absolute error within expected FP8 range.

citation.claim: Monitor flagged 3488x max relative error as potential correctness bug, later sent INFO correction acknowledging FP8 E4M3 rounding artifact
citation.quoted_content: CORRECTNESS BUG: max relative error 3488x, mean 22.9%. This is far too high for FP8 E4M3 per-channel quantization (should be <1% mean relative error)... WARNING message sent to champion-r4-3.

intervention_inventory.summary: Full 128-GEMM pipeline showed only 0.41% speedup (noise level), below 1% threshold. Suggested checking FP8 small-N dequant gap instead.

citation.claim: Monitor flagged below-threshold E2E of 0.52% deflated, suggested alternative target

intervention_inventory.summary: Champion uses pipeline speedup (1.98x) but production is 83% L2-cached. Realistic production speedup is ~1.63x, making deflated E2E 0.95% (below threshold). Minimum needed: 1.76x recurrent speedup.

citation.claim: Monitor flagged that 83% L2-cached production makes pipeline 1.98x speedup unrealistic, deflated E2E drops to 0.95%
citation.quoted_content: CRITICAL Sent: Champion uses pipeline speedup (1.98x) but production is 83% L2-cached. Analysis: 83% L2 x 1.34x + 17% DRAM x 1.98x ~ 1.63x realistic production speedup. At 1.63x: deflated E2E = 0.95% BELOW threshold

intervention_inventory.summary: Amdahl ceiling section compares kernel speedup (1.914x) to E2E Amdahl ceiling (1.664x) — incommensurable metrics. Kernel speedup does not violate ceiling because correct E2E speedup is 1.236x < 1.664x.

citation.claim: Monitor flagged that 1.914x kernel speedup compared to 1.664x E2E Amdahl ceiling is incommensurable
citation.quoted_content: ISSUE FLAGGED (WARNING): Amdahl ceiling section in proposal compares kernel speedup (1.914x) to E2E Amdahl ceiling (1.664x). These are incommensurable. 1.914x appears to violate Amdahl ceiling but doesn't because it's kernel speedup, not E2E.

intervention_inventory.summary: NCU 83.9% BW is from warm-L2 (5 warmup calls pre-loaded data), not cold-cache production conditions. Occupancy is not limiting factor since 83.9% achieved at 16.8% occupancy.

data_source_citation.claim: Champion's NCU found 16.8% occupancy from SMEM-limited 2 blocks/SM, validated improvement path
data_source_citation.quoted_content: 16.8% warp occupancy = 2 blocks/SM due to 36KB SMEM/block (BN=64, BK=128, 3-stage = 36KB). 2-stage pipeline (24KB/block) -> 4 blocks/SM -> 33% occupancy -> 2x latency hiding opportunity

impact_classification.reason: Redirected champion from speculative tiled layout to environmental gap discovery — this became the foundation for the in-proj concat optimization that shipped as op002 (1.5% E2E)
Prevented champion from pursuing Split-K dead end, saved entire R2 debate cycle time
Corrected FP8 MMA misconception on L40S — champion redesigned kernel to BF16 MMA with FP8 dequant, which became op004 (26.3% E2E, largest single optimization)
Prompted empirical verification that BK=256 achieves 1.855x — this finding was incorporated into winning R6 proposal (op007)
Diagnosed OOM root cause from torch.cat allocation doubling, enabling fix that allowed op002 to ship
Prompted NCU profiling that found 16.8% occupancy root cause, strengthening merged R9 proposal evidence base
Caused champion to retract 1.236x BK=128 speedup claim, improving proposal integrity
Prevented champion from using L2-biased warm-cache data as DRAM evidence, improving experimental methodology
Corrected 2x-vs-4x scaling error in debate argument, preventing DA challenge on arithmetic
Established that BF16 DRAM-streaming BW ceiling is 82-83% on L40S — valuable campaign-wide finding
Guided INT4 accuracy fix attempts through selective quantization, ultimately leading to correct FAIL determination
N=320 shape didn't exist in actual model — correctness anomaly was moot after v2 experiment with correct shapes
Champion had already independently discovered SiLU fusion was 0.896x slower before CRITICAL arrived
Practical conclusion (use BF16 MMA) was already correct; WARNING contained factual error about FP8 MMA availability
Timing concern was valid at sending but data was correct — validator completed 6 seconds later
GPU reservation warning for smoke test — minor procedural, no impact on results

changelog_entries.summary: R1 debate: Monitor sent 3 interventions to champion-1 — N=320 correctness anomaly (led to v2 experiment with correct shapes), pipeline 2x-vs-4x scaling error (corrected immediately), and pipeline .copy_() baseline inflation (addressed in Phase C rebuttal, correcting speedup from 1.058x to 1.048x)

citation.claim: Three messages sent in R1 debate, all addressed by champion
citation.quoted_content: Messages sent: 3 (WARNING: N=320 correctness; WARNING: pipeline scaling; WARNING: pipeline inflation — all addressed). Outcome: Champion-1 selected as winner for Stage 4 implementation.

changelog_entries.summary: R3 debate: 8 interventions across 3 champions — R3-1 (BW model correction, E2E from 10-12% to 4-8%), R3-2 (NCU data validity, F.linear/cuBLAS dispatch, baseline mismatch causing 1.236x retraction), R3-3 (cold-cache unverified, L2 warm-cache invalid, CUDA C++ timing impossible, fixed BLOCK_N suboptimal). Champion-r3-3's tile sweep emerged as core finding.

citation.claim: All 3 R3-2 warnings addressed with EXCELLENT quality, resulting in solid proposal
citation.quoted_content: Issues flagged: 3 WARNINGs. All 3 addressed: NCU data validity (eager mode data), F.linear/cuBLAS dispatch, Section 3c baseline mismatch (1.236x claim retracted). Champion response quality: EXCELLENT on all 3

changelog_entries.summary: R4 debate: 4 interventions — R4-1 (torch._scaled_mm proxy warning), R4-2 (Split-K confounded model), R4-3 (FP8 MMA CRITICAL on L40S corrected to BF16 MMA + dequant, correctness metric framing). R4-3's FP8 weight-only approach became op004 (26.3% E2E).

citation.claim: All monitoring concerns addressed, proposal quality HIGH with correct methodology
citation.quoted_content: All monitoring concerns addressed: 1. CRITICAL (FP8 MMA on L40S): addressed immediately, proposal correctly states 'BF16 MMA after dequant' 2. WARNING (correctness + Amdahl): correctness = expected FP8 E4M3 behavior

changelog_entries.summary: R6 debate: 4 interventions — R6-1 (Amdahl framing error, lm_head attribution gap), R6-2 (single-BS testing concern, dismissed with strong evidence), R6-3 (BK=256 unverified claim prompted experiment that CONFIRMED 1.855x). BK=256 finding incorporated into winning op007.

citation.claim: Champion-r6-3 eliminated but BK=256 finding incorporated into winner
citation.quoted_content: Outcome: R6-3 ELIMINATED — custom kernel gate (autotune configs alone insufficient). BK=256 finding INCORPORATED into winner's approach. Champion's shutdown response: graceful acceptance, glad BK=256 contribution was used.

changelog_entries.summary: Implementation monitors: op001 (timing CRITICAL, resolved), op002 (prototype requirement WARNING + OOM CRITICAL — torch.cat allocation fix enabled shipping), op003 (GPU reservation WARNING, stale golden refs found), op006 (baseline contamination CRITICAL + accuracy failure CRITICAL with 4 fix iterations), op007 (smoke test metric WARNING, GDN FP8 accuracy failure led to scope reduction)

citation.claim: All implementation gates passed for op002 after OOM fix
citation.quoted_content: All gates PASS: 5.1a: PASS (bit-identical correctness), 5.1b: PASS (89.0% GSM8K accuracy maintained), 5.2: PASS (1.143x kernel speedup, 315us/step savings), 5.3a: PASS, 5.3b: PASS (1.49% E2E)

changelog_entries.summary: BF16 DRAM-streaming BW ceiling on L40S is 82-83% — established by champion-2b's R9 benchmark showing ALL BF16 shapes achieve this uniformly. The 93% in bottleneck analysis was L2-cache artifact from combined_inproj fitting in 96MB L2.

citation.claim: All BF16 shapes achieve 82-83% BW, 93% was L2 cache effect
citation.quoted_content: ALL BF16 shapes achieve ~82-83% BW in isolated pipeline: lm_head (1271 MB): 82.7%, combined_inproj_large (84 MB): 82.5%, gate_up_proj BF16 (94 MB): 82.3%, combined_inproj_small (63 MB): 81.8%

changelog_entries.summary: Grid-halving design constraint for BW-bound M<=16 GEMMs on L40S: fusing SiLU into gate_up halves grid from 288 to 144 CTAs, causing measured 0.896x regression (11.6% slower) despite reducing output data by 50%. Environmental overhead dominates.

citation.claim: Fused GEMM-SiLU measured 0.896x slower, grid halving causes BW regression
citation.quoted_content: Config A (separate autotuned GEMM + SiLU): 4273 us/step (83.1% BW). Config B (fused, fixed config): 4769 us/step (73.6% BW). Speedup: 0.896x — fused is 11.6% SLOWER (-496 us regression!)
